answer:
We are given the parametric equations $x=3\sin t$ and $y=\cos t+2\sin t$. We can eliminate the parameter $t$ by solving for $\cos t$ in the second equation and substituting into the first equation.

We have that $\cos t=y-2\sin t$. Substituting this into the first equation, we get
\begin{align*}
x&=3\sin t\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \cos t&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y-2\sin t&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y-2\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y-\frac{2x}{3}&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y&=\frac{5x}{3}.
\end{align*}
We can now substitute this expression for $y$ into the equation $y=\cos t+2\sin t$ to get
\begin{align*}
\frac{5x}{3}&=\cos t+2\sin t\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{1-\sin^2t}+2\sin t\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^2}+2\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{1-\frac{x^2}{9}+2\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{\frac{9-x^2+6x}{9}}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\frac{\sqrt{9-x^2+6x}}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 15x&=\sqrt{9-x^2+6x}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 225x^2&=9-x^2+6x\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 226x^2-x^2+6x&=9\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 225x^2-x^2+6x&=9\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 224x^2+6x&=9\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 224x^2+6x-9&=0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 16x^2+x-9&=0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad (16x+9)(x-1)&=0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad x=-\frac{9}{16}\text{ or }x=1.
\end{align*}
We can now substitute these values of $x$ into the equation $y=\frac{5x}{3}$ to get
\begin{align*}
y&=\frac{5\left(-\frac{9}{16}\right)}{3}=-\frac{15}{16}\text{ or }y=\frac{5(1)}{3}=\frac{5}{3}.
\end{align*}
We can now substitute these values of $x$ and $y$ into the equation $ax^2+by+cy^2=4$ to get
\begin{align*}
a\left(-\frac{9}{16}\right)^2+b\left(-\frac{15}{16}\right)+c\left(-\frac{15}{16}\right)^2&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{81}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{306}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{306}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=\frac{1024}{256}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{306}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=\frac{1024}{256}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 306-15b+225c&=1024\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 306-15b+225c&=1024\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -15b+225c&=1024-306\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -15b+225c&=718\\
\end{align*}
and
\begin{align*}
a(1)^2+b\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)+c\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^2&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad a+\frac{5b}{3}+\frac{25c}{9}&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 9a+15b+25c&=36\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 9a+15b+25c&=36\\
\end{align*}
We can now solve this system of equations to get
\begin{align*}
-15b+225c&=718\\
9a+15b+25c&=36\\
\end{align*}
Multiplying the first equation by $-1$ and adding it to the second equation, we get
\begin{align*}
-15b+225c&=718\\
9a+15b+25c&=36\\
\hline
9a+225c&=36-718\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 9a+225c&=-682\\
\end{align*}
Multiplying the first equation by $-1$ and adding it to the second equation, we get
\begin{align*}
-15b+225c&=718\\

We have that $\cos t=y-2\sin t$. Substituting this into the first equation, we get
\begin{align*}
x&=3\sin t\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \cos t&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y-2\sin t&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y-2\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y-\frac{2x}{3}&=\frac{x}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad y&=\frac{5x}{3}.
\end{align*}
We can now substitute this expression for $y$ into the equation $y=\cos t+2\sin t$ to get
\begin{align*}
\frac{5x}{3}&=\cos t+2\sin t\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{1-\sin^2t}+2\sin t\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^2}+2\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{1-\frac{x^2}{9}+2\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\sqrt{\frac{9-x^2+6x}{9}}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{5x}{3}&=\frac{\sqrt{9-x^2+6x}}{3}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 15x&=\sqrt{9-x^2+6x}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 225x^2&=9-x^2+6x\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 226x^2-x^2+6x&=9\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 225x^2-x^2+6x&=9\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 224x^2+6x&=9\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 224x^2+6x-9&=0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 16x^2+x-9&=0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad (16x+9)(x-1)&=0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad x=-\frac{9}{16}\text{ or }x=1.
\end{align*}
We can now substitute these values of $x$ into the equation $y=\frac{5x}{3}$ to get
\begin{align*}
y&=\frac{5\left(-\frac{9}{16}\right)}{3}=-\frac{15}{16}\text{ or }y=\frac{5(1)}{3}=\frac{5}{3}.
\end{align*}
We can now substitute these values of $x$ and $y$ into the equation $ax^2+by+cy^2=4$ to get
\begin{align*}
a\left(-\frac{9}{16}\right)^2+b\left(-\frac{15}{16}\right)+c\left(-\frac{15}{16}\right)^2&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{81}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{306}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{306}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=\frac{1024}{256}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{306}{256}-\frac{15b}{16}+\frac{225}{256}c&=\frac{1024}{256}\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 306-15b+225c&=1024\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 306-15b+225c&=1024\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -15b+225c&=1024-306\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -15b+225c&=718\\
\end{align*}
and
\begin{align*}
a(1)^2+b\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)+c\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^2&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad a+\frac{5b}{3}+\frac{25c}{9}&=4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 9a+15b+25c&=36\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 9a+15b+25c&=36\\
\end{align*}

-15b+225c&=718\\
